collected.policy_snippet: if you choose not to save any voice recordings, voice IDs associated with your account will be deleted and voice ID will not work. Similarly, if you delete the voice recordings Alexa used to create your voice ID
collected.purpose: Voice IDs are created from voice recordings to enable voice identification functionality within Alexa services.

data_minimization.reasoning: The policy does not describe concrete limits on what biometric data (voice IDs) is collected, nor does it justify why each element is strictly necessary. It broadly collects voice recordings and derives voice IDs without specifying scope or minimization measures.

stored.policy_snippet: if you choose not to save any voice recordings, voice IDs associated with your account will be deleted and voice ID will not work. Similarly, if you delete the voice recordings Alexa used to create your voice ID
stored.purpose: Voice IDs derived from voice recordings are stored to enable personalized voice identification for Alexa services.

retention_policy.policy_snippet_deletion: if you choose not to save any voice recordings, voice IDs associated with your account will be deleted and voice ID will not work. Similarly, if you delete the voice recordings Alexa used to create your voice ID (by, for example, choosing to have your Alexa voice recordings deleted automatically after 3 or 18 months), voice ID may not work.

biometric_data.overall_privacy_risk_description: The policy confirms that biometric voice IDs are created and stored from user voice recordings, but does not specify a legal basis for this processing or impose clear data minimization limits. Deletion of voice IDs is tied to user-initiated deletion of voice recordings, meaning biometric data persists until the user actively requests removal, posing a risk for users unaware of this linkage.

data_minimization.reasoning: The policy does not mention health data collection, so there are no data minimization measures described for this category.
The policy does not mention collection of physiological data, so no data minimization assessment can be made for this category.

collected.policy_snippet: Our physical stores may use cameras, computer vision, sensors, and other technology to gather information about your activity in the store, such as the products and services you interact with.
collected.purpose: Physical data such as images and videos are collected in physical stores to gather information about customer activity, including interactions with products and services.

data_minimization.reasoning: The policy describes broad collection of physical data including images, videos, location information, and in-store activity via cameras, computer vision, and sensors, without specifying meaningful limits on what is collected or clear justifications for why each specific type is necessary.

stored.policy_snippet: images and videos collected or stored in connection with Amazon Services
stored.purpose: Images and videos collected in connection with Amazon Services are explicitly stated to be stored.

shared.policy_snippet: These third-party service providers have access to personal information needed to perform their functions, but may not use it for other purposes.
shared.purpose: Physical data as part of personal information may be shared with third-party service providers who perform functions on Amazon's behalf, such as fulfilling orders, delivering packages, and analyzing data.

physical_data.overall_privacy_risk_description: Amazon collects extensive physical data including images, videos, location information, and in-store activity through cameras, computer vision, and sensors, without specifying clear retention periods or deletion rules for this category. The broad and vague scope of collection, combined with sharing with third-party service providers and no explicit data minimization commitments for physical data, presents significant privacy risks for users.

collected.policy_snippet: Automatic Information: We automatically collect and store certain types of information about your use of Amazon Services, including information about your interaction with products, content, and services available through Amazon Services.
collected.purpose: Amazon collects behavioral data such as browsing history, purchase history, content interaction, clickstream data, and app usage to provide, improve, and personalize Amazon Services, as well as for advertising and fraud prevention.

data_minimization.reasoning: The policy describes broad and extensive collection of behavioral data including full URL clickstreams, purchase and content use history, content interaction details, device metrics, application usage, and interactions with Alexa, without providing meaningful limits or specific justifications for why each type is necessary. The scope of collection appears expansive rather than minimal.

stored.policy_snippet: purchase and content use history, which we sometimes aggregate with similar information from other customers to create features like Top Sellers
stored.purpose: Behavioral data such as purchase history and content use history is stored to create aggregated features, personalize recommendations, and improve Amazon Services.

retention_policy.policy_snippet_deletion: Yes, you can choose to automatically delete your voice recordings, text transcripts, and typed requests to Alexa after a specified period of time (3 or 18 months), or you can choose to not save any voice recordings.
retention_policy.policy_snippet_inactivity: If you choose automatic deletion after 3 or 18 months, we will delete the voice recordings, text transcripts, and typed requests associated with a chat once the specified period of time has passed since your last request to Alexa within that chat.

shared.policy_snippet: We provide ad companies with information that allows them to serve you with more useful and relevant Amazon ads and to measure their effectiveness. We never share your name or other information that directly identifies you when we do this. Instead, we use an advertising identifier like a cookie, a device identifier, or a code derived from applying irreversible cryptography to other information like an email address.
shared.purpose: Behavioral data linked to advertising identifiers is shared with ad companies to serve relevant ads and measure their effectiveness.

behavioral_data.overall_privacy_risk_description: Amazon collects extensive behavioral data including browsing history, purchase history, content interactions, clickstreams, and Alexa interactions, and shares pseudonymized behavioral data with third-party ad companies. While some deletion controls exist for Alexa interactions, other behavioral data lacks clear deletion timelines. Additionally, even after deletion, Amazon may retain systems trained on user data and other records of interactions, posing ongoing privacy risks.
